Rename Files Automatically

Turn messy filenames into clear, searchable names automatically.
Rename My Files Automatically

Why file names become messy

Many files have names like IMG_4938.jpg, Scan0001.pdf, Document_Final_v7.pdf, or Screenshot(14).png.

After months or years of downloading and creating files, these names make it difficult to find important documents.

Poor filenames slow down searches and create unnecessary clutter.

Automatically rename files with AI

Foldora analyzes files and generates meaningful filenames automatically.

  • Replace generic names with descriptive titles
  • Improve file search and discoverability
  • Rename documents, images, and PDFs
  • Preview changes before applying them
  • Works completely offline

Instead of manually renaming hundreds of files, Foldora can process them in seconds.

Examples of automatic file renaming

  • IMG_4938.jpg → Team Meeting Photo.jpg
  • Scan0001.pdf → Employment Contract.pdf
  • Screenshot(15).png → Monthly Sales Dashboard.png
  • Document_Final_v7.docx → Project Proposal.docx

Meaningful filenames make your files easier to browse and search.

How it works

  1. Select a folder
  2. Analyze files
  3. Preview suggested names
  4. Apply changes safely

You always review changes before anything is renamed.

Benefits of automatic renaming

  • Find files faster
  • Improve folder organization
  • Reduce duplicate-looking filenames
  • Save time compared to manual renaming
  • Create cleaner archives and folders

Is it safe?

Foldora never uploads files to external servers.

All processing happens locally on your computer and every rename operation can be previewed beforehand.

Frequently Asked Questions

Can I rename hundreds of files at once?
Yes. Foldora can process entire folders automatically.

Does it work with PDFs and images?
Yes. Foldora can rename many common file types.

Do I need an internet connection?
No. Everything runs locally on your machine.
